title: facility — retention windows
description: Facilities are permanent while any tenant references them. Bookings + day-passes + passes are financial records retained 7 years (SOC 2 CC6.3 + local tax requirements + PCI-DSS 10.7). Anonymous day-pass profile fields (name + phone) are redacted 90 days post-consumption per GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) minimisation.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(c) — data minimisation (anonymous day-pass redaction)
gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure (with financial-retention carve-out)
pci-dss 10.7 — audit trail retention 1 year hot + 6 years archive
national tax law — 7-year financial-record retention (US, EU, UK, CA baselines)

hot.days: while any booking / day_pass / pass references it (RESTRICT FKs enforce)

purge.trigger: manual archive by admin + zero downstream references + 7 years past deleted_at
purge.notes: The observer refuses hard-delete while any downstream row exists. Retention window aligns with the longest-lived downstream row (booking / day_pass / pass), all of which retain 7 years.

hot.description: Active + first year for dispute resolution + support queries

cold.description: Financial-audit retention. Soft-deleted after 1 year; hard-purged after 7.

purge.trigger: > 7 years past ends_at + status IN (completed / cancelled / no_show)
purge.notes: Retention runs against ends_at, not created_at, because a booking's financial significance is realised at its end. Cancelled bookings past ends_at still retain 7 years.
NO SoftDeletes on day_passes — retention is straight hot→purge.

Preserves receipt_number + price_amount_cents + payment_method for financial retention. Only the PII sub-fields are redacted, not the whole row. Registered-user day passes (issued_to_user_id NOT NULL) are unaffected — their profile lives on the User row and follows the User's retention.

purge.trigger: > 7 years past valid_until (or revoked_at when revoked)
purge.notes: Soft-deleted on revoke; hard-purged 7 years past. Financial retention applies because passes tie to Membership revenue.

purge.action: cascaded via the facility row (blackouts are a JSONB sub-field, not a separate table)
purge.notes: Historical blackout data is inspectable via the audit trail's before/after snapshots on FacilityBlackoutAdded / FacilityBlackoutRemoved events.

Every pricing_json change writes to the audits table with before/after snapshots + 7-year audit retention (financial). The current pricing_json lives on the facility row; historical values live in the audit trail.

cross_reference: The audit rows written by Auditable outlive the source rows per audit::retention.json — audit retention is 7 years regardless of source-row retention.
TenantErased cascades hard-delete of every facility row + downstream (FK cascade), but the audit rows written during those events survive the cascade (per audit module's outlives-source design).
